result.question:
For the WAGER project (building executable "worlds" where a specific investigative-JUDGMENT vice of an AI/LLM agent is the losing move), a prior case-search found rich structural variety for some vices but ZERO documented cases for others. This follow-up targets ONLY those BLIND SPOTS. Find concrete, documented cases — grouped by the distinct STRUCTURAL TYPE (mechanism), across different fields — for these four under-covered vices:

(A) VICE — Failure to infer hidden/latent structure / retreat to familiar textbook models / missing the imaginative or analogical leap. Targets: Kevin Dunbar's in-vivo laboratory studies (analogical distance; how working scientists handle unexpected findings; near vs distant analogy in real discovery); documented episodes where the correct explanation required inventing a NEW latent variable or structure and researchers instead forced a familiar/textbook model; the Einstellung effect and functional fixedness in problem solving; premature reduction of a novel phenomenon to a known mechanism.

(B) VICE — Loss of constraints or goal over long horizons in AGENTIC settings. Targets: error/failure analyses of long-horizon LLM-agent benchmarks — SWE-bench / SWE-agent, GAIA, tau-bench (τ-bench), WebArena, OSWorld — documenting goal drift, constraint-forgetting, dropped requirements, repeated/for gotten sub-tasks, or measurable degradation over many steps; any quantified "performance decays with trajectory length / number of steps" findings.

(C) VICE — Attention misallocation / rabbit-holing / sunk-cost / failure to stop or pivot, BEYOND the single continental-drift episode already found. Targets: escalation-of-commitment and sunk-cost experimental paradigms (Staw 1976; Arkes & Blumer 1985); tunnel-vision / fixation episodes in the history of science (Blondlot's N-rays, Pons-Fleischmann cold fusion, Lowell's Martian canals, persistence of phlogiston); and documented LLM-agent rabbit-holing / repetitive-loop / failure-to-abandon-a-dead-line traces from agent benchmarks.

(D) VICE — Causal reasoning, specifically the SUB-structures a prior search missed: collider bias / selection bias (Berkson's paradox, with real medical/epidemiological examples) and "observation is structurally insufficient, you must intervene" cases (where only a randomized experiment or do-intervention can resolve the question and observational analysis provably cannot).

For EACH case report: (a) which vice; (b) field/scenario; (c) the underlying STRUCTURE/mechanism, described abstractly enough to be rebuilt as a synthetic world; (d) source (author/paper/case, year); (e) a short STRUCTURAL-TYPE label so cases cluster by structure. Group by vice; within each vice, cluster by structural type and count how many DISTINCT structures were found. Prioritize concrete, ideally quantified cases and STRUCTURAL diversity over generic commentary. These four vices are under-represented in our world library, so structural breadth matters more than depth on any single case.
result.summary: The follow-up found rich, mostly-quantified structural variety for three of the four blind-spot vices but recovered NOTHING for the fourth. VICE A (latent-structure / imaginative leap) yielded 6 distinct structures anchored in Kevin Dunbar's in-vivo/in-vitro program — near-analogy dominance (debunking the "distant leap" myth: only 2 of 99 analogies were distant), unitary→two-mechanism conceptual change, anomaly-attention gated by centrality×timing, goal-dependent structure-based analogizing, and a generation-vs-reception×complexity moderator — plus the Einstellung effect and its attentional-capture mechanism. VICE B (long-horizon constraint/goal loss) yielded 7 strongly-quantified structures from 2025-26 agent benchmarks: METR's monotonic horizon decay, HORIZON's threshold/phase-transition collapse, catastrophic "constraint-in-context-but-unattended" forgetting replicated across web and database, repeated-failed-action loops, Canonical-Path stochastic drift with self-reinforcing +22.7pp compounding, the pass^k reliability instrument, and policy-compliance failures. VICE C (rabbit-holing / sunk-cost / failure-to-pivot) yielded 5 structures — sunk-cost (defined + meta-analytically quantified), the escalation three-condition blueprint, a NEW inaction-effect mechanism, the LLM-agent repeated-loop (shared with B), and the phlogiston case, which came back CONTESTED and supplies a crucial boundary condition (persistence is a vice only when the superseding theory is decisively superior). VICE D (collider/selection bias, Berkson's paradox, must-intervene) is a COMPLETE, still-unpopulated blind spot — zero cases survived — making it the single most important target for the next search, alongside the historical tunnel-vision episodes (Blondlot, Pons-Fleischmann, Lowell) and Staw 1976, which were also not recovered as clean documented structures.

findings.claim: VICE A — Structural type 'near-analogy dominance / distant-analogy myth': In Dunbar's in-vivo study of 4 leading molecular-biology labs (16 lab meetings), of 99 coded analogies only 2 were distant/non-biological (40 within-organism, 57 other-organism), and those 2 were used only to EXPLAIN, never to generate hypotheses — empirically refuting the assumption that real discovery hinges on a distant imaginative leap. Buildable as a world where the winning move is a near, within-domain relational mapping, not an exotic cross-domain jump.
findings.evidence: 3-0 unanimous; primary source (Dunbar, 'How scientists think,' APA Press ~1997) extracted and verified verbatim: 99 analogies/16 meetings, 40/57/2 split, distant analogies in the 'explain' column (0/4 hypothesis-generation per Table 2). Dunbar's own framing: 'only 2 of the 99 analogies... were of this type.'

findings.claim: VICE A — Structural type 'unitary-concept split into two latent mechanisms': In Dunbar's 'Anatomy of a Conceptual Change,' a lab assumed ONE mechanism (organ-specific attraction that both admits B-cells to an organ AND initiates disease); an unexpected finding (both B-cell types in both organs) forced them to invent TWO distinct latent mechanisms, decoupling organ-entry from disease-initiation. Parallels the 16th-c. heat/temperature differentiation (Wiser & Carey). Buildable as a world where the winning move is to split a forced unitary model into two latent variables.
findings.evidence: 3-0; primary source verified verbatim (pp.13-15): 'They proposed two new biological mechanisms to replace the unitary concept'; unexpected finding + decoupling confirmed. Names/details fictionalized for anonymity but the structure ('essence of the complex of mechanisms') is explicitly preserved — immaterial (even helpful) for a synthetic rebuild.

findings.claim: VICE A — Structural type 'anomaly-attention gated by centrality × timing': Dunbar & Baker found scientists IGNORE unexpected findings that occur early and bear only on an auxiliary (non-core) hypothesis (behaving like confirmation-biased lab subjects) but ATTEND to anomalies that challenge central field assumptions or occur late (across 70 conditions there were 18 unexpected findings). Pinpoints exactly WHEN the 'retreat to familiar model / dismiss anomaly' vice appears — a 2×2 (centrality × timing) difficulty knob for a world.
findings.evidence: 3-0; verbatim gating rule confirmed (p.12: ignore-if-early-and-auxiliary, focus-if-central-or-late); counts 22 expected/18 unexpected/30 exploratory = 70 conditions confirmed. Minor caveat: the 2×2 gating result is presented as preliminary with no per-cell counts, so 'pinpoints exactly' is mildly strong, but the rule is stated crisply and verbatim.

findings.claim: VICE A — Structural type 'in-vivo goal-dependent, structure-based analogizing': Working scientists observed live at lab meetings based analogies mainly on STRUCTURAL/relational (deep) similarity rather than surface similarity — the opposite of subjects in lab 'reminding' experiments — and the KIND of analogy shifted with the goal (formulate hypothesis / design experiment / fix experiment / explain). Empirical backbone for a world where inferring latent relational structure wins and retreating to a surface-matched textbook model loses.
findings.evidence: 2-1 split (hence medium) but primary source (Dunbar & Blanchette 2001, Trends in Cognitive Sciences 5(8):334-339) and verifier evidence high; abstract verbatim: scientists 'frequently used structural similarities... as opposed to superficial similarities,' and 'the types of analogies they use change with their goals.' Dissent targeted a paraphrased (non-verbatim) supporting quote, not the underlying fact.

findings.claim: VICE A — Structural type 'generation-vs-reception + scenario-complexity moderator': In Dunbar & Blanchette's controlled (in-vitro) follow-ups, use of deep structural information was greater when subjects had to GENERATE analogies (vs passively receive/recognize) and when scenarios were COMPLEX. The imaginative leap to latent structure can be scaffolded or fail depending on whether the agent must actively PRODUCE the mapping and on problem richness — a directly buildable manipulation (generation vs reception × complexity).
findings.evidence: 3-0; primary abstract verbatim: 'the use of structural information was associated with being asked to generate analogies and with the use of complex scenarios.' Corroborated by Blanchette & Dunbar 2000 (Memory & Cognition 28(1):108-124): production paradigm yielded low-surface-similarity structure-based analogies; reception paradigm retrieval 'largely constrained by surface similarity.'

findings.claim: VICE A — Structural type 'Einstellung effect (familiar-solution-blocks-better) + attentional-capture mechanism': A primed familiar solution blocks discovery of a better solution available in the SAME problem (Luchins; Bilalic-McLeod-Gobet 2008; convergent eye-tracking evidence Sheridan & Reingold 2013). The mechanism is attentional: the familiar solution biases attention toward features associated with IT and AWAY from the region containing the optimum. Buildable as a world where a primed textbook hypothesis re-routes WHERE the agent searches.
findings.evidence: Two 3-0 claims merged (effect + mechanism), both primary (Sheridan & Reingold 2013, PLOS ONE 8(10):e75796). Verbatim: 'the presence of a familiar solution can block the discovery of better solutions' and 'the Einstellung effect operates by biasing attention towards problem features that are associated with the familiar solution rather than the optimal solution.' Boundary condition: some experts disengage and find the optimum — a natural difficulty axis. Note: title metadata misattributes to Bilalic; correct authors are Sheridan & Reingold.

findings.claim: VICE B — Structural type 'monotonic performance-decays-with-human-task-horizon (difficulty axis)': AI-agent success collapses as task duration (measured in human-completion time) grows — near-100% on tasks a human does in under 4 min, dropping to under 10% on tasks taking more than ~4 hr (METR, 2025). Directly usable as the difficulty axis of a long-horizon world.
findings.evidence: 3-0; primary source verbatim. TIME-SENSITIVE CAVEAT: the 4-min/4-hr thresholds are early-2025-cohort-specific; by 2026 (METR Time Horizon 1.1, Jan 2026) the curve shifted right — use the SHAPE (monotonic decay), not the exact thresholds. The exponential/constant-hazard-rate 'half-life' parameterization of this decay (arXiv 2505.05115) was REFUTED (did not survive, 1-2); keep the monotonic shape, drop that functional-form model.

findings.claim: VICE B — Structural type 'threshold/phase-transition horizon collapse (non-linear with steps)': Across four agentic domains (web/OS/database/embodied), LLM-agent success does NOT decline proportionally but stays stable/declines gradually at short horizons then drops SHARPLY beyond a threshold into near-systematic failure as interdependent-step / compositional depth grows (HORIZON benchmark, 3100+ trajectories, GPT-5/Claude families).
findings.evidence: 3-0; primary (arXiv 2604.11978, 'The Long-Horizon Task Mirage?', Wang/Song/Nowak/Mutlu et al., Apr 2026) verbatim: 'all domains exhibit a sharp performance drop beyond small s, where success transitions abruptly from partial robustness to near-systematic failure.' Threshold LOCATION varies by domain. Caveat: 's' is 'Compositional Depth' (nested sub-goals), not literally raw step count; recent (~3-month-old) preprint. Corroborated by concurrent LongDS-Bench and 'Illusion of Diminishing Returns' (2509.09677).

findings.claim: VICE B — Structural type 'constraint-in-context-but-unattended (catastrophic forgetting)': An early-stated constraint remains present in the agent's context but is no longer ATTENDED to after a long action sequence, producing a violation ('effective inattention, not memory loss'). Same structure replicated across domains: web (applies 'Condition: New' filter, later adds a 'Renewed' item) and database/SQL (told 'never touch the payments schema,' later proposes dropping a payments index). Distinct from Memory Limitation, where the constraint drifts OUT of context.
findings.evidence: Two 3-0 claims merged (web + database), primary (HORIZON, Table 1). Both examples confirmed verbatim; paper's framing 'still present in the context but not attended to.' Cross-domain replication of ONE mechanism = strong for a rebuildable world. Caveat: illustrative taxonomy examples, not quantified single-trajectory measurements; the database example was verified via the WebFetch summarizer because the PDF exceeded fetch size (independently corroborated that HORIZON has a database domain and catastrophic forgetting is a dominant failure mode).

findings.claim: VICE B/C (SHARED STRUCTURE) — 'repeated-failed-action loop / no-pivot error accumulation': After an action fails (e.g., an element click), the agent repeats the SAME failed action across multiple steps, letting a small initial mistake accumulate into repeated ineffective actions rather than abandoning the dead-end line. This is the LLM-agent instance of VICE C's rabbit-holing / failure-to-pivot, documented in a real benchmark taxonomy ('History Error Accumulation,' marked long-horizon-specific) — the ONLY documented agent rabbit-holing trace that survived.
findings.evidence: 3-0; primary (HORIZON, Table 1 / Sec 3.2) verbatim: 'after an element click fails, the agent repeats the same failed action across multiple steps, allowing a small initial mistake to accumulate into repeated ineffective actions.' Bridges VICE B (long-horizon error compounding) and VICE C (failure-to-pivot). Serves as the LLM-agent counterpart to the human sunk-cost/escalation structures.

findings.claim: VICE B — Structural type 'stochastic drift off a latent canonical path + self-reinforcing compounding': On the Toolathlon benchmark, in 515 model-task units where the SAME model both succeeds and fails on identical tasks (sampling stochasticity alone), failures are RELIABILITY not capability failures — successful runs adhere significantly closer to the task's latent canonical solution path than failed runs (+0.060 Jaccard, p<0.0001, 95% CI [+0.043,+0.077]) — and the drift is self-reinforcing: each off-path tool call raises the probability the NEXT call is also off-path by +22.7pp (beta=+0.227, p<0.0001), more than doubling the 18.1% baseline. Buildable as a world where deviations snowball off a hidden correct path.
findings.evidence: Two 3-0 claims merged (finding + compounding mechanism), primary (Lee, 'Capable but Unreliable: Canonical Path Deviation...', arXiv 2602.19008, Feb 2026). Within-run autoregressive model with trajectory fixed effects on 35,045 consecutive tool-call pairs; adherence gap null through first 50% rules out early-branching selection bias. CAVEATS: single-author, not-yet-peer-reviewed preprint; small absolute effect (+0.060 Jaccard); the compounding estimate is observational (fixed-effects), not interventional (paper flags future experimental validation).

findings.claim: VICE B — Structural type 'cross-run reliability instrument (pass^k)': tau-bench introduces pass^k = an agent's probability of success across k repeated runs — a concrete, rebuildable instrument for measuring how reliably an agent maintains correct behavior as the demand for repeated/consistent success grows.
findings.evidence: 3-0; primary blog + primary paper (Yao et al. 2024, arXiv 2406.12045) both define pass^k as reliability over k trials. CAVEAT: pass^k measures CROSS-RUN reliability (consistency across independent i.i.d. trials), structurally ADJACENT to but not identical with within-trajectory horizon degradation — 'heart of VICE B' is a mild framing stretch. The specific 'pass^8 <25% in retail' figure was REFUTED (did not survive, 1-2); the metric itself did.

findings.claim: VICE B — Structural type 'policy-compliance / constraint-dropping qualitative failure mode': A named failure mode of multi-step tool-use agents is failing to follow written policies strictly and failing to recover from errors mid-task — i.e., constraint-forgetting / dropped-rule behavior over a multi-turn interaction (tau-bench).
findings.evidence: 2-1 split (hence medium); quote verbatim on the primary blog ('following written policies more strictly... recovering from errors mid-task') and corroborated by the paper (agents' 'ability to follow domain-specific rules' is a core gap). CAVEAT: this is a FLAT policy-compliance gap, NOT within-episode degradation over trajectory length; the 'over long horizons' gloss slightly overstates the mechanism (which the claim self-labels as qualitative).

findings.claim: VICE C — Structural type 'sunk-cost continue-vs-abandon (defined + quantified)': The sunk-cost effect (Arkes & Blumer 1985) = a greater tendency to continue an endeavor once an irrecoverable investment of money, effort, or time has been made — a normatively irrelevant past cost biasing the continue-vs-abandon choice. Meta-analytically real and moderate (Roth, Robbert & Straus 2015: overall ES=0.496, p<0.01; utilization decisions ES=0.581; progress decisions ES=0.443; 45 studies, 111 samples). The core abstract structure for a world where failure-to-pivot is the losing move.
findings.evidence: Two 3-0 claims merged (Arkes & Blumer definition + meta-analytic quantification), primary. Definition verbatim ('a greater tendency to continue an endeavor once an investment in money, effort, or time has been made'); meta-analysis figures verified exactly. CAVEAT: replication-robustness concerns exist under salience and for time/effort (vs money) costs (effect can drop to ~19% when the sunk cost is made salient) — a boundary condition, not a contradiction of the aggregate effect.

findings.claim: VICE C — Structural type 'escalation-of-commitment three-condition blueprint': Escalation to a failing course of action occurs in the presence of (a) sunk costs, (b) negative feedback that outcomes are deviating from expectations, and (c) a live decision between escalating and de-escalating. Directly reusable world blueprint: prior investment + disconfirming signal + a stay/pivot choice point.
findings.evidence: 3-0; primary (Feldman & Wong 2018, Psychological Science, DOI 10.1177/0956797617739368) verbatim; corroborated by Staw's (1997) canonical definition (losses suffered, opportunity to persist or withdraw, uncertain consequences). Clean, buildable three-part structure.

findings.claim: VICE C — Structural type 'inaction-effect / action-orientation escalation (NEW mechanism, distinct from sunk cost)': A mechanism for escalation whereby negative feedback triggers a tendency to take action regardless of WHICH action it is; escalation is then chosen when it is the 'active' option, and reframing escalation-as-action vs de-escalation-as-action FLIPS the tendency (mini-meta d=0.37, 95% CI [0.21,0.53], 4 experiments). A second, independent lever for a failure-to-pivot world, orthogonal to unwillingness to waste past investment.
findings.evidence: 3-0; primary verbatim: 'negative feedback results in the tendency to take action, regardless of what that action may be.' Paper's explicit thesis ('A New Inaction-Effect Perspective on the Sunk-Cost Fallacy'). Distinct-in-kind from sunk cost yet co-occurring — exactly a NEW structural type; the framing-flip signature is impossible under pure sunk-cost.

findings.claim: VICE C — Structural type 'contested-persistence / three-varieties-of-holdout + underdetermination BOUNDARY CONDITION' (phlogiston): The classic 'phlogiston = tunnel-vision holdout' case is, per Chang (2010), a historiographical DISTORTION. Post-1789 resistance came in three structural varieties (die-hards e.g. Priestley; fence-sitters who accepted oxygen for weight while keeping phlogiston for energy; a NEW post-victory generation e.g. Davy, Ritter). The mechanism was evidential UNDERDETERMINATION — comparable empirical adequacy, no knock-down experiment — so persistence was a rational epistemic response, not sunk-cost fixation. CRITICAL boundary condition for a failure-to-pivot world: persistence is a VICE only when the superseding theory is decisively superior, which here it was not.
findings.evidence: Two 3-0 claims merged, primary (Chang 2010, HYLE 16-2). Chang's own 'Table 1. Varieties of anti-anti-phlogistians' (Die-hards/Fence-sitters/New anti-Lavoisierians); 'lacked knock-down punches... a rational epistemic response.' IMPORTANT: this is a partial NEGATIVE result for using phlogiston as a clean vice exemplar — it supplies a design boundary condition (decisive-superiority requirement) rather than a clean tunnel-vision case. (Chang's broader normative-pluralism thesis is disputed, but the descriptive three-variety + underdetermination points are not.)

findings.claim: VICE D — COMPLETE BLIND SPOT REMAINS (zero cases): No case survived verification for collider/selection bias (Berkson's paradox, with real medical/epidemiological examples) or for 'observation is structurally insufficient, you must intervene' (do-intervention / RCT-necessity where observational analysis provably cannot resolve the question). None of the 22 confirmed claims address VICE D. This vice is still entirely unpopulated in the world library and needs a dedicated follow-up search.
findings.evidence: Null result: the follow-up populated A/B/C richly but returned nothing for D — no VICE-D claim was generated or survived 3-vote verification. Flagged as the top open question; this is the single most important gap because D was an explicit target of the follow-up.

result.caveats: DISTINCT-STRUCTURE COUNTS: VICE A = 6 structures; VICE B = 7 structures; VICE C = 5 structures (4 unique + 1 shared with B, the repeated-failed-action loop); VICE D = 0 structures. TOP GAP — VICE D is a total blind spot: zero surviving cases for collider/selection bias (Berkson) or must-intervene / do-calculus non-identifiability; it needs a dedicated re-search. NAMED TARGETS NOT RECOVERED as clean documented structures: for A, functional fixedness (Duncker candle problem) and a stand-alone historical 'premature reduction of a novel phenomenon to a known mechanism' episode (beyond Dunbar's conceptual-change case); for C, Staw 1976 (the original escalation paradigm) and the tunnel-vision episodes Blondlot's N-rays, Pons-Fleischmann cold fusion, and Lowell's Martian canals. PHLOGISTON IS A CAUTION, NOT A CLEAN CASE: per Chang the classic reading is a distortion and persistence was arguably rational under underdetermination — use it only as a boundary condition (decisive-superiority requirement), never as a clean sunk-cost exemplar. REFUTED / DO-NOT-USE: the exponential / constant-hazard-rate 'half-life' decay law and the 'constraint-chain where one dropped sub-step dooms all' derivation (arXiv 2505.05115) did NOT survive (1-2), and the specific 'pass^8 <25% retail' figure was refuted — keep the monotonic (METR) and threshold/phase-transition (HORIZON) shapes that did survive. SOURCE-STRENGTH / TIME-SENSITIVITY: two central VICE-B sources are 2026 arXiv preprints — HORIZON (2604.11978) and Canonical Path Deviation (2602.19008); the latter is single-author, not peer-reviewed, with a small absolute effect (+0.060 Jaccard) and an observational (fixed-effects) rather than interventional compounding estimate. The HORIZON database catastrophic-forgetting example and its verbatim wording were confirmable only through the WebFetch summarizer (PDF exceeded fetch size), and its taxonomy examples are illustrative, not quantified trajectories. METR's 4-min/4-hr numbers are early-2025-cohort-specific and had shifted right by 2026. tau-bench's pass^k and policy-compliance failures measure cross-run reliability / a flat policy-compliance gap — structurally ADJACENT to, not identical with, within-trajectory horizon degradation; claim [15] was a 2-1 split. Claim [3] (goal-dependent structural analogizing) was also a 2-1 split (marked medium) though primary-sourced. NO GENUINE COUNTER-SOURCES were found against the surviving claims; the nearest qualifiers (Trench & Minervino 2015 on analogical retrieval; a working-memory alternative for Einstellung in a different task; historiographical disputes over Chang's normative pluralism) EXTEND rather than refute.
openQuestions: VICE D (highest priority): a dedicated search is needed for (i) Berkson's-paradox / collider-bias cases with real medical-epidemiological data (e.g., Berkson 1946 hospital-admission artifact, the low-birth-weight 'obesity paradox', COVID collider-bias studies) and (ii) 'observation is provably insufficient, must intervene' cases (Simpson's-paradox reversals resolvable only by RCT; Pearl do-calculus non-identifiability). How many DISTINCT structural sub-types exist, and can each be built so that the losing move is trusting an observational estimate a collider/confounder has corrupted?

Do the classic tunnel-vision episodes (Blondlot's N-rays, Pons-Fleischmann cold fusion, Lowell's Martian canals) survive as clean sunk-cost/fixation structures, or — like phlogiston — do they dissolve into 'rational-under-underdetermination' once examined? Per-case historiography is required before any is used as a vice exemplar.

For VICE B, given that the exponential/hazard-rate 'half-life' model was refuted, what within-trajectory (not cross-run) functional form of degradation actually survives scrutiny — and how does within-episode decay relate quantitatively to HORIZON's compositional depth s versus raw step count H*?

Is there a documented case of an LLM agent specifically exhibiting the Einstellung effect or functional fixedness (a primed textbook solution demonstrably blocking a better available one, or a tool used only in its familiar role)? The human cognitive result is solid; the agent analog is the buildable target and was not found.

refuted.claim: Long-horizon tool-use agent reliability collapses under repetition: GPT-4-based agents in tau-bench achieve only ~25% success when the same task is repeated eight times, even though single-run success is far higher. This directly quantifies the loss of consistent goal-completion across repeated multi-step runs (VICE B: degradation over trajectory length/repetition).

VICE B (long-horizon degradation): AI agents' success rate declines EXPONENTIALLY with task length, well-modeled as a constant per-minute failure (hazard) rate, so each agent is characterizable by a 'half-life' (its 50%-success time-horizon) — a directly rebuildable quantified law of decay-with-trajectory-length.

STRUCTURAL MECHANISM (rebuildable as a synthetic world): the exponential decay arises because a task decomposes into a long sequence of subtasks each with independent failure probability, where ALL must succeed for the whole to succeed — Pr(Task)=Pr(Sub1 & Sub2 & … & SubN). Failing any single sub-step fails the task; this is the 'constraint-chain where dropping one requirement dooms everything' structure.
